success.description: Validation flag indicating successful data extraction from all sources

mrn.description: Medical Record Number - unique patient identifier used as primary key

demographics.description: Basic patient information extracted from most recent MD notes

diagnosis.description: Comprehensive cancer diagnosis and disease status information

fields.metastatic_sites: Array of organs/locations where cancer has spread
fields.disease_status: Current disease behavior using standard oncology terminology (e.g., 'Progressive Disease (PD)', 'Stable Disease (SD)')

comorbidities.description: Non-cancer medical conditions and functional status

fields.comorbidities: Array of condition objects with name, severity, clinical details, and medications

treatment_tab_info_LOT.description: Lines of Therapy view - structured treatment history with line numbering

fields.treatment_history: Array of treatment line objects containing header (line number, drug name, status), dates, systemic regimen (drug names only), local therapy (radiation/surgery), cycles data, toxicities, outcome with clinical details, and reason for discontinuation

treatment_tab_info_LOT.key_concept: Line numbers only for systemic therapies. Line changes on disease progression or complete therapy switch. Dosage changes keep same line number.

treatment_tab_info_timeline.description: Chronological timeline view of all major cancer-related treatment events

fields.timeline_events: Array of events in reverse chronological order with date display, systemic regimen, local therapy, context-specific clinical details, and event type (Systemic/Radiation/Surgery/Imaging)

diagnosis_header.description: Structured summary of key diagnosis information for UI display

fields.primary_diagnosis: Full cancer name with proper medical terminology
fields.metastatic_sites: Array of spread locations (excludes primary organ)

diagnosis_evolution_timeline.description: Stage evolution and disease progression timeline with normalized dates

fields.timeline: Array of progression events with normalized date labels (e.g., 'December 2025'), event descriptions, stage info, and clinical details

diagnosis_footer.description: Additional diagnosis context and duration calculations

lab_info.description: Structured laboratory test results with clinical interpretation

fields.tumor_markers: CEA, NSE, proGRP, CYFRA 21-1 with values, units, dates, status, reference ranges
fields.complete_blood_count: WBC, Hemoglobin, Platelets, ANC with values, units, dates, status, reference ranges
fields.metabolic_panel: Creatinine, ALT, AST, Total Bilirubin with values, units, dates, status, reference ranges
fields.clinical_interpretation: Array of abnormality summaries (anemia, hepatic dysfunction, neutropenia rules)

lab_info.critical_rule: ONLY extracts from formal lab reports, IGNORES MD notes. Uses 'Lab Resulted' dates, not collection dates.

lab_reports.description: Google Drive URLs linking to raw laboratory report documents (5-11 per patient)

genomic_info.description: Clinically relevant molecular and genomic profiling data
genomic_info.source: Genomic testing reports or MD visit notes

fields.driver_mutations: Status of 9 key oncogenic drivers (EGFR, ALK, ROS1, KRAS, BRAF, MET, RET, HER2, NTRK) with detection status, variant details, and actionability flag
fields.additional_alterations: Only FDA-approved targets, guideline-recommended alterations, or clinical trial-relevant findings

genomic_info.key_concept: Focuses on actionable findings. Driver mutations always reported even if not detected. Additional alterations filtered for clinical relevance.

genomics_reports.description: Google Drive URLs linking to genomic testing and molecular profiling reports (1-6 per patient)

pathology_summary.description: Primary pathology diagnosis and procedural details

diagnosis_section.full_diagnosis: Array of 5-7 KEY bullet points (concise, proper capitalization)

pathology_markers.description: Advanced pathology features including morphology and immunohistochemistry (IHC) markers

fields.morphology_column: Context-dependent: Tissue architecture (for resections) or Cellular features (for cytology/FNA)

ihc_column.markers: Array of IHC markers with standardized names, status (Positive/Negative/Equivocal/Focal), and details (intensity + quantity)

fields.interpretation_tags: 3-5 diagnostic signature tags (histologic subtype, driver status, high-risk features)

pathology_markers.special_handling: Splits combined marker lists (e.g., 'CK7 and TTF1 positive' → two separate marker objects)

pathology_reports.description: Google Drive URLs linking to pathology and biopsy report documents (1-7 per patient)

radiology_reports.description: Google Drive URLs linking to radiology imaging reports (CT, MRI, PET scans) with extracted structured data

extracted_sections.report_summary: Study type, study date, overall response, prior comparison date
extracted_sections.recist_measurements: Dual-baseline tracking: compares current scan vs BOTH initial diagnosis baseline AND current treatment baseline with % changes for each target lesion
extracted_sections.impression: Array of distinct findings from impression section
extracted_sections.additional_findings: Top 3-5 clinically relevant findings beyond impression (new lesions, non-target changes, incidental findings)

radiology_reports.special_feature: Dual-baseline RECIST allows tracking disease response relative to two different time points simultaneously

data_quality_standards.extraction_principle: Deterministic (temperature=0), explicit data only, no inference or hallucination
data_quality_standards.null_handling: Returns null for missing data, never fabricates values
data_quality_standards.validation: Schema enforcement with strict formatting rules

formatting_rules.TNM_classification: NEVER includes the word 'Stage' (e.g., 'T2a N1 M0')
formatting_rules.disease_status: Use RECIST terms (CR, PR, SD, PD) or clinical standard terms
